column1: # Disposition manifest for the lxp syscall surface — the classification the coverage
# guard (scripts/syscalls/check-syscalls.sh) freezes against drift. Every LXP_NR_* in
# include/lxp/lxp_syscall.h must have exactly one row here; adding a syscall without
# classifying it fails CI. The guard also proves each row still matches the source:
#   implemented | benign-stub | refused-eopnotsupp  -> must be a `case LXP_NR_x` in src/lxp_syscall.c
#   run-loop-handled                                 -> intercepted in src/lxp_run.c, NOT a dispatch case
# "benign-stub" = accepted and answered with a fixed/inert result on purpose (the tier
# does not need the real semantics); "implemented" = does the syscall's real work.
# Columns: <name> <TAB> <disposition> <TAB> <note>.  Lines starting with # are ignored.

column3: reaps / blocks via the run loop
captured; the run loop loads the image
NET-gated; iovec gather, no ancillary data
